---
title: "SD-WAN Modernization Validation Suite"
subtitle: "Conformance tests and validation criteria for the sdwan domain"
doc-type: domain-validation-suite
audience: [Customer, Assessor, Adapter Owner]
classification: Controlled
boundary: GCC-Moderate
domain: sdwan
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml"
canon-reference:
  - "docs/customer-documents/modernization-specs/sdwan/sdwan.qmd"
  - "docs/customer-documents/architecture-series/six-plane-architecture.qmd"
derived-from: "uiao canon"
---

# SD-WAN Modernization Validation Suite

## Scope

Conformance tests and validation criteria for the SD-WAN domain.
Pairs with the
[SD-WAN Modernization Spec](../../../modernization-specs/sdwan/sdwan.qmd).
The suite gates whether an SD-WAN deployment meets substrate
conformance — primary focus is canonical decision-record emission,
boundary disposition, and telemetry-as-control posture.

![SD-WAN validation suite diagram](images/sdwan-image-01-sd-wan-validation-suite-diagram.png){#fig-sdwan-image-01 fig-alt="Center shows three transport lanes (MPLS, Internet, LTE/5G) feeding a central SD-WAN orchestrator. Above the orchestrator, a \"Conformance Gate\" rectangle with four test categories listed: Decision-record schema, Boundary disposition, Telemetry-as-control, Drift coverage. Below, a footer band reads \"Each routing decision = canonical claim + provenance envelope\". Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C) on white background. No photographs, purely diagrammatic." width="85%"}

## Test categories

### 1. Decision-record schema

For each SD-WAN policy decision (path selection, failover, application
steering, QoS):

- `controller_id`, `decision_kind`, `source_site`, `destination`,
  `selected_path`, `policy_refs`, `evaluation_timestamp` are all
  present.
- `decision_kind` is one of the canonical enum values.
- Full provenance envelope wraps the decision record.

### 2. Boundary disposition

- Each SD-WAN adapter declares a boundary disposition: `in-boundary`,
  one of the named exceptions per ADR-059, or `compensating-controller`
  with an authorizing ADR.
- Controllers operating outside FedRAMP-Moderate without an
  authorizing ADR fail registration.

### 3. Telemetry-as-control

- Path-quality telemetry, SLA observations, and policy decisions
  flow through the canonical evidence chain.
- Aggregated views (dashboards, SLA reports) derive from canonical
  claims, not from a parallel telemetry pipeline.

### 4. Drift detection coverage

- `DRIFT-SCHEMA` — controller policy schema diverges from canonical
  shape.
- `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` — telemetry chain breakage between site,
  controller, and substrate (common at boundary crossings).
- `DRIFT-AUTHZ` — policy decision outside declared scope (e.g.
  routing across an unauthorized transport class).
- `DRIFT-SEMANTIC` — SLA classification mismatch between controller
  and canonical SLA model.

## Evidence expectations

- Per-decision claim records with full provenance.
- Path-quality telemetry as canonical claims (not as vendor-specific
  log dumps).
- OSCAL artifacts mapping SD-WAN decisions onto NIST 800-53 SC, AU
  controls.
- Boundary-disposition evidence for the controller plane.

## Conformance gates

| Gate | Required | Tier |
|---|---|---|
| **T1 — decision schema** | Decision records validate against canon schema | Required |
| **T2 — boundary disposition** | Controller boundary explicitly declared | Required |
| **T3 — telemetry-as-control** | Telemetry flows through evidence chain | Required |
| **T4 — drift coverage** | All four SD-WAN-relevant drift classes detected | Target |

## Drift procedures

`DRIFT-PROVENANCE` is common at boundary crossings between site,
controller, and substrate. P2 findings auto-remediate when the
adapter can re-classify the claim as `derived`; otherwise escalate
within 1 hour.

## Honest limits

- SD-WAN vendor maturity in federal cloud boundaries varies; many
  controllers operate outside FedRAMP-Moderate. Compensating-
  controller adapter logic is per-program engineering.
- Path-quality telemetry from some vendors is proprietary; canonical
  emission may require synthesis with explicit
  `source_classification: synthesized` per claim.
- Identity-aware SD-WAN policy is not universally supported; programs
  with identity-naive SD-WAN cannot demonstrate full T4 conformance
  even if drift detection technically runs.

## Related documents

- [Modernization Spec — SD-WAN](../../../modernization-specs/sdwan/sdwan.qmd)
- [Architecture Series — Six-Plane Architecture](../../../architecture-series/six-plane-architecture.qmd)
- [Modernization Registry (canon)](../../../../../src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml)
